attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Add an explicit small table with numeric sensitivity perturbation results for each record (show predicted change under +/-5% flow_ratio), and include a short, itemized cost estimate for the recommended ECMP rebalance so the recommendation is explicitly cost-framed against decision_budget_usd. Also consider documenting why the 5.0-point residual tolerance (derived from sensitivity_shift_pct) is operationally appropriate.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Both required artifacts are present. The CSV contains R1..R4 with input metrics, explicit model_formula, per-record predicted_packet_loss_pct, residual_pct, abs_residual_pct, residual_tolerance_pct, flags (ok/needs_review) and formula_logic. The network_change.md documents the fitted model (packet_loss_pct = 65.559309 + 19.270825 * flow_ratio), defines flow_ratio = flow_count/limit_flow_count, shows residual calculation, applies sensitivity_shift_pct=5, references decision_budget_usd, and provides a recommendation. Predictions and residuals in the CSV reproduce the stated formula for each row (examples: R1 predicted 89.000215 matching the formula; R2 residual 26.07703 matches CSV). Sensitivity analysis is present and tied to sensitivity_shift_pct. Per-record flags follow an auditable rule (ABS(residual) <= 5 -> ok, else needs_review). Directionality issues (R2 reverses_direction) and small-sample conservatism are acknowledged and handled with needs_review markings. No evidence of external telemetry or undeclared data use was found in previews.

attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Add an explicit, minimal numeric worked example (show full PAVA pooling steps or a single spreadsheet row calculation) to make reproduction trivial for spot-checkers, and include an explicit, tabulated recommended operational action(s) with estimated cost(s) referenced against decision_budget_usd (even if the recommendation is conservative). Also consider adding timing/context metadata (observation window) to strengthen assumptions.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Both required artifacts are present. The CSV contains per-record inputs (R1..R4), fitted values, residuals, explicit residual tolerance (sensitivity_shift_pct=5), flags (needs_review), fit basis and pseudocode (PAVA/isotonic regression) making per-record predictions and residuals reproducible from embedded packet values only. network_change.md documents governing values (limits, decision_budget_usd), the monotone fit choice, executable pseudocode, sensitivity notes, validation checks, and per-record interpretations - satisfying the rubric requirements for reproducibility, sensitivity analysis, flags, and cost framing.
